sources.name: Mega Man 3 ROM Disassembly — Matrixz / Trax
sources.notes: Annotated 6502 disassembly. Source for walk speed fixed-point representation, jump velocity, and slide mechanics.

sources.name: Data Crystal — Mega Man 3
sources.notes: RAM map for physics variable addresses.

sources.name: Speedrun.com — Mega Man 3 Resources
sources.notes: Speedrunning physics documentation. Jump height comparison with MM2 and invincibility frame counts confirmed here.

sources.name: SDA Knowledge Base — Mega Man 3
sources.notes: Game mechanics documentation. Jump velocity difference from MM2 (-5.5 vs -6.0 px/frame) is the most cited MM2→MM3 change.

notes: Mega Man 3 uses the same 8.8 fixed-point physics engine as Mega Man 2.
KEY DIFFERENCE FROM MM2: Jump initial velocity is lower (-5.5 vs -6.0 px/frame). MM3 jumps are noticeably shorter.
All other physics (gravity, max fall, slide speed/duration, invincibility) are identical to MM2.
MM3 introduced Proto Man mid-stage duels and the Rush search system.
Rush Jet changed from MM2's auto-scrolling platform to a directional rider-controlled vehicle in MM3.
Slide mechanic is inherited from MM2. Same speed, same hitbox reduction, same duration.

mm2_vs_mm3_comparison.source: Speedrunning community documentation and ROM disassembly comparison

differences.significance: HIGH — most impactful physics difference. MM3 jumps are clearly shorter. Some MM2 routes are impossible in MM3.

subpixel_system.description: Same 8.8 fixed-point system as MM2. All velocities are multiples of 16 sub-pixels.

walk_speed.notes: Identical to MM2. Instant velocity, no acceleration.

ground_movement.notes: Instant velocity as in MM2. No acceleration/deceleration curves.

initial_y_velocity.source: SDA KB and speedrun community: MM3 jump velocity is -5.5 px/frame, versus MM2's -6.0 px/frame. This is the most-cited physics difference between the two games.
initial_y_velocity.notes: Jump initial velocity is lower than MM2. The difference is clearly perceptible in gameplay — MM3 Mega Man cannot reach some platforms reachable in MM2.

variable_jump.mechanic: Releasing jump early reduces height. Same mechanic as MM2.
variable_jump.notes: Variable height jump through early button release.

gravity.notes: Identical to MM2. 0.25 px/frame² is the series constant for NES Mega Man.

hitbox.notes: Slide hitbox is reduced in height. Standard standing hitbox is ~24px tall; slide reduces to ~16px. The exact offset of the hitbox top during slide needs ROM verification.

slide.notes: Identical to MM2 in speed (3.0 px/frame) and duration (22 frames). Total slide distance: 66px = 4.125 tiles.

invincibility_frames.notes: Identical to MM2. 128-frame invincibility is the NES Mega Man series standard.

rush_jet_mm3.description: Rush Jet in MM3 is fundamentally different from MM2. It's now a directional rider-controlled vehicle.
rush_jet_mm3.altitude_hold: Rush Jet maintains altitude with no input (no drift down)
rush_jet_mm3.control: Left/right D-pad for horizontal, Up/Down for altitude
rush_jet_mm3.energy_drain: Energy depletes over time while in use
notes: MM3's Rush Jet is vastly more useful than MM2's auto-scrolling version.
The directional control enables precise maneuvering around obstacles.
Max horizontal speed matches walk speed (1.5 px/frame) — Rush Jet doesn't provide a speed advantage.

rush_marine.gravity: suspended while in Rush Marine — no drift
rush_marine.notes: Rush Marine provides underwater mobility matching Rush Jet's surface capability. Not used in speedruns due to limited stage application.

mega_buster.notes: Standard series values. MM3 does NOT have the Mega Buster charge shot (added in MM4).

proto_man_duels.notes: Proto Man appears between stages for mid-game fights. Proto Man uses the same physics engine as Mega Man — identical walk speed, jump velocity, and gravity.

momentum_burst.notes: Top Spin activation gives a brief horizontal momentum addition. This is weapon-specific velocity, not a base physics constant.

sliding.notes: Approximately half standing height. Exact offset needs ROM verification.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The 0.5 px/frame lower jump velocity in MM3 vs MM2 is imperceptible to most players but critical to level designers. When building MM3-inspired levels, measure jump clearances against the MM3 max of ~48px (3 tiles), NOT MM2's ~52px.

The Rush Jet directional control transforms it from a gimmick into a true movement tool. The level design around Rush Jet in MM3 reflects this — it's used for actual traversal, not just cute one-off sections.
The lower jump in MM3 makes slide+jump combos more important as movement tools, since vertical reach is reduced. Level design should reward horizontal mobility.
Proto Man duels use standard physics — this means the AI opponent is bound by the same physical constraints as the player, making the fights feel grounded and consistent.
